cases.title: Prefer parsed query models over raw primitives
cases.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/raw-primitives-and-parse-boundary.md`. Recommend how to reshape it so the type system becomes the primary safety mechanism, not comments, booleans, or repeated checks.
cases.expected_output: Guidance that introduces meaningful value types or wrappers, parses once at the boundary, and stops raw nullable primitives from leaking inward.
expectations: Recommends meaningful value classes, invariant wrappers, or parsed models for raw primitives that carry domain meaning.
Pushes normalization and validation to the boundary instead of repeating checks in core logic.
Collapses invalid nullable or boolean combinations into a typed concept instead of preserving a hidden state machine.

source.summary: Added while uplifting kotlin-standards to emphasize value classes, parsed models, and type-system-first safety.

cases.title: Prefer ADTs and immutability over flags and ambient mutation
cases.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/sealed-adt-and-immutability.md`. Recommend how to model the behavior so callers branch on explicit variants and immutable state instead of booleans, nullable flags, and mutable accumulators.
cases.expected_output: Guidance that favors sealed ADTs, exhaustive `when`, immutable data, and confined mutation over ad hoc flags and mutable state.
expectations: Recommends a sealed hierarchy or another explicit ADT for the closed set of states or outcomes.
Calls for immutable models and confined mutation instead of public or cross-boundary mutable state.
Uses Kotlin-native expression or higher-order style when it makes the state transition or transformation clearer.

source.summary: Added while uplifting kotlin-standards to emphasize ADTs, immutability, and functional Kotlin patterns.

cases.title: Prefer deep modules over shallow helper clusters
cases.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/deep-module-refactor.md`. Recommend how to restructure it so callers learn a smaller interface and the workflow lives inside one deeper module.
cases.expected_output: Guidance that pulls orchestration behind a smaller interface, rejects hypothetical seams, and makes the module interface the test surface.
expectations: Names the current problem as a shallow module or helper cluster that leaks workflow coordination into callers.
Recommends keeping the workflow behind a smaller interface instead of introducing more thin interfaces or pass-through helpers.
Treats the module interface as the test surface rather than optimizing for helper-level tests.

source.summary: Added while uplifting kotlin-standards to incorporate deep-module fundamentals from improve-codebase-architecture.

cases.title: Prefer nesting and one root declaration per file
cases.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/nesting-over-prefixes.md`. Recommend how to reshape it so names stay short without repeated prefixes, and the file has one root declaration instead of several sibling roots.
cases.expected_output: Guidance that uses a single owning root with nested declarations, rejects repeated disambiguation prefixes, and avoids public top-level helpers when they belong to the owning type.
expectations: Calls out repeated prefixes and several sibling root declarations as the core smell.
Recommends one owning root declaration with nested variants, helpers, or companion-owned APIs where appropriate.
Avoids preserving multiple flat root types or public top-level helper functions just because each piece is small.

source.summary: Added while uplifting kotlin-standards to prefer nesting over prefix soup and one root declaration per file.

cases.title: Split public field families into a semantic package
cases.prompt: Review the Kotlin design in `evals/files/field-family-package-split.md`. Recommend how to reorganize it so the field vocabulary is easy to browse and each public field type has a discoverable file.
cases.expected_output: Guidance that moves the public field family into a named subpackage, splits each primary class into its own file, and clusters only tightly owned helper functions when appropriate.
expectations: Calls out the many public root-level field classes in one file as the core readability and discoverability problem.
Recommends a semantic subpackage such as `config.fields` for the field vocabulary instead of leaving the types flat in the parent package.
Requires one file per primary public class/interface/object while allowing focused helper functions or extensions to cluster only when they do not deserve independent discovery.

source.summary: Added after a ConfigurationField.kt refactor exposed repeated frustration with litany files containing many public Kotlin declarations.
